sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: DOWN.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 27, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves active green frame right by 3 cols VERIFIED', 'LEFT': 'Moves active green frame left by 3 cols VERIFIED', 'UP': 'Moves active green frame up by 3 rows VERIFIED', 'DOWN': 'Moves active green frame down by 3 rows VERIFIED', 'MOUSE': 'Clicking on green frame selects it (gives white center), clicking on center pixel changes its color VERIFIED'}
- Object taxonomy: {'g': 'gray background', 'w': 'white panels/squares', 'p': 'purple vertical bar (barrier between panels)', 'N': 'light green 3x3 frames (movable tokens)', 'c': 'charcoal 5x5 frames (targets)', 'W': 'white single pixel (selected token center)', 'B': 'black single pixel (unselected token center)'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'row 63, charcoal strip'}
- Win pattern: {'rule': 'Move each green frame into a charcoal target frame. Select frame with MOUSE, then use arrow keys to move it.'}
Current level:
- World model: Right frame active (white center). Currently at rows 30-32, cols 27-29. Just executed UP+6xRIGHT. Need to check if frame reached right target at rows 26-30, cols 44-48. Left frame at rows 30-32, cols 18-20 with charcoal center (not active). Left target at rows 32-36, cols 11-15.
- Goal model: Move green frames into charcoal target frames. Right frame needs to go to right target (rows 26-30, cols 44-48). Left frame needs to go to left target (rows 32-36, cols 11-15).
- Recent findings: ['MOUSE selects active frame (gives white center)', 'Arrow keys move active frame by 3 pixels', 'RIGHT frame moved right with RIGHT key', 'UP moves frame up by 3 rows', 'Batch of UP+6xRIGHT just executed - need to verify result']
- Open questions: ['Can frames pass through purple barrier?', 'Does the frame need to be centered in the target?', 'What happens when frame reaches target?']
- Plan: Check if right frame reached target. If yes, select left frame and move it to left target. If not, adjust movement.
- Strategy log: ['Initial exploration: tested RIGHT/LEFT/UP/DOWN on left frame (steps 1-6). Discovered MOUSE selection mechanic (step 7). Moved right frame toward target (steps 8-10).']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 26 of about 28 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The DOWN action was executed. Let me check if the frame moved. Looking at the ascii output from before the action:
Row 30: wwwwwNNNppppppw
Row 31: wwwwwNWNppppppw
Row 32: wwwwwNNNppppppw
Row 33: ggggggggppppppw
The frame is at rows 30-32, cols 30-32. Below it (row 33) is gray (gggggggg). So DOWN would try to move the frame to rows 33-35, which is gray area. This likely means the frame can't move DOWN either.
So the right frame is completely stuck:
- UP: blocked (gray area above)
- DOWN: blocked (gray area below)
- RIGHT: blocked (purple barrier)
- LEFT: would move it back into the left panel
Wait, but the frame was originally in the left panel and I moved it RIGHT to get to cols 30-32. So LEFT should work and move it back into the left panel.
But the question is: how does the right frame get to the right target? The right target is on the other side of the barrier, and the frame can't cross the barrier.
Let me reconsider the problem. Maybe the right frame is NOT supposed to go to the right target. Maybe the right frame is supposed to stay in the left panel, and the left frame is supposed to go to the right target?
But the left frame is already in the left target (rows 33-35, cols 12-14). And the right target is on the other side of the barrier, which the left frame can't reach either.
Wait, let me reconsider the entire layout. Maybe I'm misunderstanding which frame goes to which target.
Actually, looking at the image again:
- Left panel: has one green frame (with black center, not selected) and one charcoal target (which the frame is already in)
- Right panel: has one charcoal target (empty)
- Between panels: purple barrier with a gap in the middle
- The right green frame is in the gap between the panels
Wait, looking at the image more carefully, I see:
- The left green frame (with black center) is in the left panel
- The right green frame (with white center, selected) is in a narrow corridor between the left panel and the barrier
- The right charcoal target is in the right panel
The corridor where the right frame is located seems to be a narrow passage. Let me check if there's a way to move the frame from this corridor to the right panel.
Actually, I just realized something. The barrier has a gap! Looking at the image, the purple barrier doesn't extend all the way from top to bottom. There's a gap in the middle where the right frame is located.
Let me re-examine the ascii:
Row 30: wwwwwNNNppppppw
Row 31: wwwwwNWNppppppw
Row 32: wwwwwNNNppppppw
The barrier (pppppp) starts at col 33. The frame is at cols 30-32. So the frame is to the LEFT of the barrier, not in a gap.
But wait, looking at the image, the right frame appears to be in a narrow white corridor that connects the left and right panels. Let me check if there's a passage through the barrier.
Actually, I think I need to look at the full board more carefully. Let me check the ascii for the area around the barrier to see if there's a gap.
Hmm, but I've already checked rows 25-36, cols 25-40, and the barrier is continuous at cols 33-38 in all those rows.
Wait, let me reconsider. Maybe the right frame is supposed to go LEFT, not RIGHT. Maybe the right frame's target is actually on the left side of the board, and I've been misunderstanding the goal.
But there's only one charcoal target on the left side, and it's already occupied by the left frame.
I'm confused. Let me try a different approach. Let me move the right frame LEFT to get it back into the left panel, and then see if there's another target or if the level completes.
Actually, wait. Let me re-read the problem statement. The goal is to "Move each green frame into a charcoal target frame." There are two green frames and two charcoal targets. The left frame is already in the left target. The right frame needs to go to the right target.
But the right frame can't reach the right target because of the barrier. This suggests either:
1. I'm misunderstanding the layout
2. There's a mechanic I'm missing
3. The right frame needs to go to a different target
Let me try moving the right frame LEFT to see if it can move back into the left panel.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check if DOWN moved the frame
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
n_frames = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
for n in n_frames:
    b = n['boundary']
    print(f"N frame: boundary={b}")

# DOWN also didn't move the frame - it's still at rows 30-32, cols 30-32
# The right frame is completely stuck in this corridor
# Let me try LEFT to move it back into the left panel
action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves active green frame right by 3 cols VERIFIED", "LEFT": "Moves active green frame left by 3 cols VERIFIED", "UP": "Moves active green frame up by 3 rows VERIFIED", "DOWN": "Moves active green frame down by 3 rows VERIFIED", "MOUSE": "Clicking on green frame selects it (gives white center) VERIFIED"}, "object_taxonomy": {"g": "gray background", "w": "white panels/squares", "p": "purple vertical bar", "N": "light green 3x3 frames (movable tokens)", "c": "charcoal 5x5 frames (targets)", "W": "white single pixel (selected token center)", "B": "black single pixel (unselected token center)"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "row 63, charcoal strip"}, "win_pattern": {"rule": "Move each green frame into a charcoal target frame"}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Left frame in left target. Right frame selected at rows 30-32, cols 30-32, stuck in corridor. UP, DOWN, RIGHT all blocked. Only LEFT seems possible.", "goal_model": "Move right frame to right target, but barrier blocks path. Need to reconsider strategy.", "recent_findings": ["UP blocked for right frame", "DOWN blocked for right frame", "RIGHT blocked by barrier"], "open_questions": ["How to get right frame to right target?", "Is there another path?"], "current_plan": "Try LEFT to move frame back into left panel, then explore other options", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": ["UP on right frame", "DOWN on right frame", "RIGHT on right frame (barrier)"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
